description_long: This is a small, unmanned airplane with a tan-colored fuselage and straight, rectangular wings extending from the midsection. The nose features a rounded, bulbous cockpit area with a translucent, blue-tinted canopy marked by yellow framing. The tail section is distinctive, with an H-tail configuration formed by two vertical stabilizers connected by a horizontal stabilizer, all in the same tan color. The landing gear consists of two small wheels, one under the nose and one under the fuselage, both fixed and exposed. The overall design suggests a lightweight, possibly surveillance or reconnaissance aircraft, with no visible external engines or armaments.
description: A small tan airplane with a bulbous blue-tinted cockpit, straight mid-mounted wings, and a unique H-tail. It has fixed landing gear and a lightweight, unmanned design.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the rounded cockpit canopy, straight wings, and the H-tail structure clearly.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the H-tail configuration and the fixed landing gear beneath the fuselage.
description_view_2: Rear-right view emphasizing the tail structure and the straight, rectangular wings.
description_view_3: Front-right view focusing on the bulbous cockpit canopy and the symmetry of the wings and tail.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, straight rectangular wings with no visible dihedral or winglets.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers joined by a horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed tricycle landing gear with two visible wheels, one under the nose and one under the fuselage.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single-seat, bulbous canopy featuring yellow framing.
